falsifiableQuestion: Does the search produce a Lean-verified proof that is NOT a near-duplicate of the corpus (novel: true)?

Real Lean path requires LeanDojo (stateful); not installed on host. Abstained.

verdict: BLOCKED / negative — no novel verified proof produced on this host
interpretation: Answer to the falsifiable question: NO. (1) The only proof the search found (trivial_true via the stub applier) is a near-duplicate of the corpus (char-trigram Jaccard overlap 1.0 >> 0.92 threshold) → novelty FALSE, correctly classified as retrieved/non-novel. It is also a STUB-accepted proof ('accepted-by-injected-applier'), NOT a real Lean verification. (2) zero_add and add_comm could not be proved by the stub applier and the search FAIL-CLOSED (no_proof_within_budget) — it never asserted an unproved goal. (3) The REAL Lean path (LeanDojo + Lean 4) abstained lean_unavailable because the toolchain is not installed on this host; it produced no fabricated proof. This is an honest BLOCKED result: the novelty signal cannot be exercised without (a) the Lean 4/elan toolchain + LeanDojo installed and (b) an LLM tactic proposer driving a real LeanProofSession. The fail-closed discipline is confirmed correct — a proof Lean would reject is never asserted.
noveltyProbeNote: Strict char-trigram Jaccard proxy at 0.92 threshold (production would use a sentence embedding). trivial_true's proof 'trivial' scored 1.0 overlap = exact corpus near-duplicate.
